LAW AND MOTION 301, NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021'S MOTION FOR PETITIION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION, MATTER TO BE HEARD AT 9:30 A.M. ON MAY 20, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 302 PER THE RECUSAL OF JUDGE BUSCH. JUDGE: PETER J. BUSCH, REPORTER: CAROL KAREN, CSR # 8189 301/PJB

LAW AND MOTION 302, PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021'S MOTION FOR PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS DENIED. PETITIONER CANNOT MEET ITS BURDEN OF SHOWING THAT THE PRESENT DISPUTE (PETITIONER'S TERMINATION) IS SUBJECT TO ARBITRATION BECAUSE PETITIONER CANNOT CITE TO ANY SPECIFIC PROVISION OF THE CBA THAT REQUIRES ARBITRATION OF THIS ISSUE. MOREOVER, THE EVIDENCE SHOWS THAT PETITIONER WAS HIRED INTO A TEMPORARY POSITION FROM A NEAR LIST AND THAT SHE WAS NOT BEING RETURNED AS PERMANENT EMPLOYEE. THEREFORE, PETITIONER CANNOT MEET ITS BURDEN AS TO THE ARBITRABILITY OF THIS DISPUTE. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR MAY-20-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 301

calendar.matters:
Ntc And Motion For Petitiion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS:
PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021'S Motion For Petitiion To Compel Arbitration, MATTER TO BE HEARD AT 9:30 A.M. ON MAY 20, 2011 IN DEPARTMENT 302 PER THE RECUSAL OF JUDGE BUSCH. Judge: Peter J. Busch, Reporter: Carol Karen, CSR # 8189 =301/PJB

Petitioner Service Employees International Union, Local 1021 Notice And Motion For Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-20-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PETITIONER SERVICE EMPLOYEES INTERNATIONAL UNION, LOCAL 1021 MOTION FOR PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION.

No appearances. The court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: petitioner's motion to compel arbitration is denied. Petitioner cannot meet its burden of showing that the present dispute (petitioner's termination) is subject to arbitration because petitioner cannot cite to any specific provision of the CBA that requires arbitration of this issue. Moreover, the evidence shows that petitioner was hired into a temporary position from a near list and that she was not being returned as permanent employee. Therefore, petitioner cannot meet its burden as to the arbitrability of this dispute. Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

Ntc And Motion For Petitiion To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-03-2011 Continued To May-20-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 301
